decision_summary: The chip peak temperature (89.28°C) exceeds the 85.0°C limit. The most effective immediate action is to increase the thermal conductivity of the heat spreader to enhance lateral heat dissipation away from the chip hot spot.

changes.reason: Increasing spreader conductivity improves lateral heat spreading, reducing the local temperature gradient under the chip and lowering the peak temperature.

expected_effects: More uniform temperature distribution across the heat spreader
risk_notes: Diminishing returns may occur if conductivity is increased too far without geometric changes
Material cost or weight implications if this were a physical prototype (not applicable in simulation)
